# Vendor Performance Intelligence Engine

> Multi-signal behavioral scoring engine that aggregates vendor-performance data from four ecosystem systems into a single composite risk score per vendor per tenant. Rules-driven, interpretable, tenant-scoped.

## What This Does

Answers the procurement question no existing system can: "Of our 300 active vendors, which 20 are degrading, and why?" Today, vendor performance signals live siloed across accounts payable, legal operations, and integration plumbing. A vendor that ships late invoices, silently drops webhook events, and breaches contract obligations appears as three separate 1-star problems instead of one 5-star vendor-to-re-tender. This engine correlates those signals over rolling 30/90/180-day windows, computes a weighted composite score with explainability (every score decomposes into top 5 contributing signals), and fires risk-band-change alerts ‚Äî giving procurement teams continuous vendor intelligence instead of quarterly post-mortems.

## Who This Is For

Chief Procurement Officers at mid-market manufacturers and B2B services firms with ‚Ç¨5M+ annual vendor spend and 100+ active vendors. Also relevant to Accounts Payable leads, Vendor Management Office teams, and engineering teams integrating vendor-risk into procurement workflows.

## Quick Start

1. Clone: `git clone https://github.com/kingsleyonoh/vendor-performance-intelligence-engine`
2. Install: `bundle install`
3. Configure: `cp .env.example .env` ‚Äî fill in required values (or leave ecosystem integrations as `{SERVICE}_ENABLED=false`)
4. Infra: `docker compose up -d postgres redis`
5. Seed: `bin/rails db:migrate && bin/rails vpi:setup` ‚Äî creates default tenant and prints API key
6. Run: `bin/dev`

## API Surface

All endpoints are scoped by `X-API-Key`. See `openapi.yaml` for full spec.

- `POST /api/tenants/register` ‚Äî self-service tenant registration (gated by `SELF_REGISTRATION_ENABLED`)
- `GET /api/tenants/me` ‚Äî current tenant profile
- `POST /api/tenants/me/rotate-key` ‚Äî rotate API key
- `POST /api/signals` ‚Äî push one signal or a batch
- `POST /api/signals/from-hub` ‚Äî Hub-fanned-out event ingress (HMAC-authenticated)
- `GET /api/vendors`, `POST /api/vendors`, `PATCH /api/vendors/:id`, `POST /api/vendors/:id/merge` ‚Äî vendor CRUD + merge
- `GET /api/vendors/:id/score/current`, `GET /api/vendors/:id/score/history` ‚Äî scores
- `GET /api/vendors/:id/signals`, `GET /api/vendors/:id/aliases`, `POST /api/vendors/:id/aliases` ‚Äî signal + alias inspection
- `GET /api/alerts`, `POST /api/alerts/:id/acknowledge`, `POST /api/alerts/:id/suppress`, `POST /api/alerts/:id/retry` ‚Äî alert lifecycle
- `GET /api/scoring-rules`, `POST /api/scoring-rules`, `POST /api/scoring-rules/:id/activate`, `POST /api/scoring-rules/preview` ‚Äî scoring config
- `POST /api/reports`, `GET /api/reports/:id/download` ‚Äî vendor scorecards, portfolio risk, retender candidates, trend analysis
- `GET /api/ingestion/sources`, `POST /api/ingestion/sources/:id/pull-now`, `GET /api/ingestion/runs` ‚Äî ingestion management
- `GET /api/health`, `/api/health/db`, `/api/health/redis`, `/api/health/ready` ‚Äî health checks

## Integration

All ecosystem connections are **OPTIONAL** ‚Äî the engine works standalone via manual `POST /api/signals`.

- **Notification Hub** (outbound) ‚Äî dispatches `vendor.risk_band_changed` and `vendor.risk_band_improved` events to procurement operators via email + Telegram. Env: `NOTIFICATION_HUB_URL`, `NOTIFICATION_HUB_API_KEY`.
- **Workflow Automation Engine** (outbound) ‚Äî triggers tenant-configured escalation workflows on HIGH/CRITICAL alerts. Env: `WORKFLOW_ENGINE_URL`, `WORKFLOW_ENGINE_API_KEY`.
- **Webhook Ingestion Engine** (inbound pull) ‚Äî per-vendor delivery failure rates via `GET /api/sources`, `GET /api/dead-letters`. Env: `WEBHOOK_ENGINE_URL`, `WEBHOOK_ENGINE_API_KEY`.
- **Invoice Reconciliation Engine** (Hub event subscribe + REST pull) ‚Äî financial signals (discrepancy rate, overdue rate). Env: `INVOICE_RECON_URL`, `INVOICE_RECON_API_KEY`.
- **Contract Lifecycle Engine** (NATS subscribe + REST pull) ‚Äî contractual signals (breach rate, renewal latency). Env: `CONTRACT_ENGINE_URL`, `CONTRACT_ENGINE_API_KEY`, `NATS_URL`, `NATS_CREDS_PATH`.
- **Transaction Reconciliation Engine** (REST pull) ‚Äî transactional signals (cross-processor timing variance). Env: `RECON_ENGINE_URL`, `RECON_ENGINE_API_KEY`.
- **Multi-Agent RAG Platform** (nightly REST pull) ‚Äî vendor-graph enrichment (parent companies, known contracts). Env: `RAG_PLATFORM_URL`, `RAG_PLATFORM_API_KEY`.

## Architecture

- **Tenant isolation**: every data-bearing table has `tenant_id UUID NOT NULL`; API key per tenant resolves via `X-API-Key ‚Üí Current.tenant`; all queries scoped.
- **Append-only signals**: `vendor_signals` is insert-only, partitioned monthly on `recorded_at` via pg_partman. Corrections insert a new row with `status='superseded'` on the original.
- **Derived scores**: `vendor_scores` snapshots computed from signals via rules-driven scoring (weighted categories + time-decay half-life + band thresholds). Every score captures top 5 contributors for explainability.
- **Alert snapshot freezing**: `risk_alerts.delivery_payload` captures the full event at creation; retries bind to this frozen shape ‚Äî tenant renames or vendor merges after the alert fires do not change what the Hub receives.
- **Report snapshot freezing**: `vendor_reports.tenant_snapshot` + `render_context` capture the tenant identity and score data at generation time; PDF re-downloads bind to the frozen context (required for procurement audit artifacts).
- **Rails layers**: `app/controllers ‚Üí lib/{ingestion,scoring,ecosystem,tenants,alerts,reports} ‚Üí models`. `lib/ecosystem/` is pure HTTP/NATS clients, zero app dependencies.

## Source

- Repository: https://github.com/kingsleyonoh/vendor-performance-intelligence-engine
- Documentation: ./README.md (generated via `/generate-readme` after build)
- PRD: ./docs/vendor-performance-intelligence-engine_prd.md
- API spec: ./openapi.yaml
- MCP server: ./mcp.json
- Agent context: ./AGENTS.md
